title: Child protection in schools - Ministry of Education
content:
Te tiaki tamariki ki rō kura

## Child protection in schools
There are a number of legal child protection requirements your school must follow.
- What child protection requirements your school must meet
- What checks you need for adults who come into your school

## What child protection requirements your school must meet #
Requirements include child protection policies, safety checks for children's workers and Police vetting for other staff and contractors.
These are requirements of the Children’s Act 2014 and Education and Training Act 2020.
Children’s Act 2014 – New Zealand Legislation
Schedule 4 of the Education and Training Act 2020 – New Zealand Legislation
This guide sets out the requirements your school needs to meet under the Vulnerable Children's Act.

## What checks you need for adults who come into your school #
The role of workers and others in your school will determine the types of child safety checks you need to complete. This document sets out these roles.
This poster sets out who needs to be checked before working at your school, and what steps that involves.
Te Whakarōputanga Kaitiaki Kura o Aotearoa has information about child protection responsibilities at school.
Resource centre – Te Whakarōputanga Kaitiaki Kura o Aotearoa
If you need more information about your school's obligations under the Children's Act 2014 and Education and Training Act 2020, contact us.
- School child protection policy
- Risk assessments after a Police vet
- Police vets for schools and kura
- Who needs to be safety checked at school
- Report suspected abuse or neglect of children and young people by adults
